data.text: M01/10 FELISIAN WITH A PURPLE COLOR AND LARGE BODIES OF WATER. OR SO IT
SEEMS. ATTEMPTED LANDINGS NEAR WATER AT 326.57 AND 327.60 AS WELL AS
ONE AT 113.47 ENDED ON RATHER MOUNTAINOUS AND HILLY BARREN TERRAIN. ONE
ATTEMPT AT RELIEF FROM THIS ENDED AT 190.108 FOUND WHAT LOOKEED TO
BECOME A WET LANDING NEAR AN ARCHIPELAGO OF SMALL ISLANDS. IT TURNED
OUT TO BE ICE. FOR MY EFFORT I WAS GREETED BY AN ENTHUSIASTIC ICE
HOPPER. FURTHER TRAVEL LED TO A VERY LARGE ICEBERG- SIMILAR TO THE ONE
ON FELYSIA, SIGHTED NEAR 22.28. APPROACHING CLOSER IT WAS DISCOVERED
TO BE AMID A FIELD OF SMALL POINTY ICEBERGS. TEMPERATURE AT THE EARLY
LANDINGS WAS ABOUT 18C. BUT AT THE LAST SITE (WHERE THERE WAS LIFE)
IT WAS -116C. THERE MAY BE SOME HELP FROM FHSJIM INTERNAL RADIATION?
